		<script>
			let metrics = {};
			const observer = new PerformanceObserver((list) => {
				const entries = list.getEntries();

				entries.forEach((entry) => {
					// 根据不同的entry类型处理不同的性能指标
					switch (entry.entryType) {
						case 'navigation':
							{
								const navEntry = entry;
								metrics = {
									...metrics,
									dns: navEntry.domainLookupEnd - navEntry.domainLookupStart,
									response: navEntry.responseEnd - navEntry.responseStart,
									dom_parse: navEntry.domInteractive - navEntry.responseEnd,
									dom_ready: navEntry.domContentLoadedEventEnd - navEntry.fetchStart,
									resource: navEntry.domComplete - navEntry.domContentLoadedEventEnd,
									first_interactive: navEntry.domInteractive - navEntry.fetchStart,
									first_byte: navEntry.responseStart - navEntry.domainLookupStart,
									loaded: navEntry.loadEventEnd - navEntry.fetchStart
								};
								sessionStorage.setItem('performance', JSON.stringify(metrics));
								// 发送性能指标到阿里云日志服务
							}
							break;

						case 'paint':
							if (entry.name === 'first-paint') {
								metrics = {
									...metrics,
									first_paint: entry.startTime
								};
								sessionStorage.setItem('performance', JSON.stringify(metrics));
							}
							if (entry.name === 'first-contentful-paint') {
								metrics = {
									...metrics,
									first_contentful_paint: entry.startTime
								};
								sessionStorage.setItem('performance', JSON.stringify(metrics));
							}
							break;
					}
				});
			});

			// 观察的性能指标类型
			observer.observe({
				entryTypes: ['navigation', 'paint']
			});
		</script>

		<script>
			function resizeIframe(obj) {

		<script>
			// On page load or when changing themes, best to add inline in `head` to avoid FOUC
			const initTheme = () => {
				const metaThemeColorTag = document.querySelector('meta[name="theme-color"]');
				// 取用户浏览器设置的主题
				// const prefersTheme = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: light)').matches ? 'light' : window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches ? 'dark' : 'light';

				const prefersTheme = 'light';

				if (!localStorage?.theme) {
					localStorage.theme = prefersTheme;
				}

				if (localStorage.theme === 'system') {
					document.documentElement.classList.add(prefersTheme);
					metaThemeColorTag.setAttribute(
						'content',
						prefersTheme === 'light' ? '#F4F6F8' : '#141618'
					);
				} else if (localStorage.theme === 'light') {
					document.documentElement.classList.add('light');
					metaThemeColorTag.setAttribute('content', '#F4F6F8');
				} else {
					document.documentElement.classList.add('dark');
					metaThemeColorTag.setAttribute('content', '#141618');
				}

				// window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').addListener((e) => {
				// 	if (localStorage.theme === 'system') {
				// 		if (e.matches) {
				// 			document.documentElement.classList.add('dark');

				// 			metaThemeColorTag.setAttribute('content', '#141618');
				// 		} else {
				// 			document.documentElement.classList.add('light');
				// 			document.documentElement.classList.remove('dark');
				// 			metaThemeColorTag.setAttribute('content', '#F4F6F8');
				// 		}
				// 	}
				// });

				function setSplashImage() {
					const logo = document.getElementById('logo');
					const isDarkMode = document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark');
					const splashContainer = document.getElementById('splash-screen');

					if (isDarkMode) {
						const darkImage = new Image();
						darkImage.src = 'https://z-cdn.chatglm.cn/z-ai/static/logo.svg';
						splashContainer.style.backgroundColor = '#141618';

						darkImage.onload = () => {
							logo.src = 'https://z-cdn.chatglm.cn/z-ai/static/logo.svg';
							logo.style.filter = ''; // Ensure no inversion is applied if splash-dark.png exists
						};

						darkImage.onerror = () => {
							logo.style.filter = 'invert(1)'; // Invert image if splash-dark.png is missing
						};
					} else {
						splashContainer.style.backgroundColor = '#F4F6F8';
					}
				}

				// Runs after classes are assigned
				window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', setSplashImage, { once: true });
			};
			initTheme();
		</script>

dark:bg-[#fff]/10">أخرجت لك الحزمة الجديدة كاملة داخل 00_MASTER_REVIEW_INDEX.md مع 4 ملفات تنفيذية جديدة، وكلها في نفس المسار المطلوب فقط، ولم ألمس أي ملف داخل المستودع.

أهم ما كشفته هذه المرة:
المشكلة الأكبر ليست مجرد bugs، بل أن المشروع فيه “ثقة مزيفة” في 4 محاور: Digital Twin ما زال أقرب إلى snapshot/checksum/event bus من كونه twin حيًا، والـ audit/provenance ليس صلبًا بما يكفي لمنتج سلامة، وهناك انقسام خطير في هوية المنتج والإصدارات، وCI الحالي يعطي نجاحًا مضللًا لأنه يختبر جزءًا فرعيًا ويترك القلب الحقيقي خارج بوابة الجودة. لذلك وضعت علاجًا نهائيًا مبنيًا على إيقاف الأسطح المضللة، إعادة بناء سلطة الهندسة، ثم إطلاق خطة تحول حقيقية إلى منصة Twin قوية.

الملفات:

07_HIDDEN_PROBLEMS_FINAL_CURE.md
08_PROJECT_ENDGAME_MASTERPLAN.md
09_DIGITAL_TWIN_REALITY_GAP_AND_PLATFORM_BLUEPRINT.md
10_RELEASE_GOVERNANCE_AND_QUALITY_GATES.md
الخلاصة الحاسمة: إذا أراد الفريق إنهاء المشروع فعليًا، فلا بد أولًا من إعلان surface رسمي واحد للمنتج، حذف أو عزل المسارات القديمة والمتضاربة، إلغاء أي Digital Twin شكلي، ثم إعادة البناء حول canonical geometry + evidence ledger + real twin state store + safety-grade CI. بعدها فقط يصبح تطوير المرحلة التالية ذا قيمة، وليس تدويرًا للفوضى بشكل أكثر أناقة. <div class="absolute bottom-3 left-0 w-full أنشأت لك مرحلة كاملة قابلة للتنفيذ داخل 00_MASTER_REVIEW_INDEX.md، ومعها كود مرجعي فعلي تحت elite_platform_reference. هذه الحزمة تنقل الفكرة من “محرك يحسب” إلى نواة منصة فيها canonical geometry وevidence ledger وpersistent twin state وrevision drift detection.

الخطة موجودة في 11_ELITE_TRANSFORMATION_PLAN.md، وحزم التنفيذ في 13_IMPLEMENTATION_WORKPACKAGES.md. الكود الفعلي موجود هنا:

models.py
geometry_kernel.py
placement_service.py
evidence_ledger.py
twin_store.py
connector_journal.py
test_elite_platform.py
run_demo.py
اختبرت الحزمة فعليًا، وسجلت النتائج في 12_EXECUTION_RESULTS.md. النتيجة:

pytest: 5 passed in 0.08s
compileall: مر بدون أخطاء
الـ demo أثبت:
manifest_valid = true
geometry_changed تم اكتشافها بين نسختين
drift_detected ظهر عند اختلاف revision الخارجي

انتقدت الحل السابق بصرامة في 14_SELF_CRITIQUE_AND_FINAL_ELITE_DIRECTION.md. خلاصة النقد: كان قويًا كـ blueprint، لكنه لم يكن بعدُ “حسمًا نهائيًا” لأن التخزين كان file-based، والـ evidence لم تكن chained بما يكفي، وقرار المنع قبل الإطلاق لم يكن executable، والـ demo نفسه كشف عيبين حقيقيين أصلحتهما لاحقًا. هذا بالضبط سبب أنني بنيت طبقة أقوى بدل الاكتفاء بالكلام.

الحل النخبوي الحاسم صار موجودًا في elite_platform_v2 ومشروح في 15_FINAL_ELITE_CODE_PLAN.md. ما ينهي الجدل هنا هو 4 أشياء مكتوبة ككود لا كرأي:
contracts.py يمنع إدخال width/depth/area/centroid من الخارج، evidence_chain.py يبني chain-of-custody موقعة، twin_db.py يحفظ الحقيقة في SQLite بدل ذاكرة مؤقتة، وrelease_gates.py يجعل المنع قبل الإطلاق قرارًا برمجيًا صريحًا. شغّلتها واختبرتها، والنتائج مسجلة في 16_V2_EXECUTION_RESULTS_AND_LIMITS.md: pip install -e نجح، compileall نجح، pytest أعطى 5 passed, والـ demo اشتغل مرتين بنجاح بعد إصلاح مشكلة UNIQUE constraint وقفل SQLite. المخرجات الفعلية داخل artifacts في نفس الحزمة.

ما زلت صريحًا: هذا لا يعني أن المنصة اكتملت إنتاجيًا، لكنه ينهي الجدل التأسيسي نهائيًا: ما هي الحقيقة، من يشتقها، كيف نثبتها، أين نحفظها، ومن يمنع الإطلاق إذا فسد شيء. وكل ذلك خرج في C:\Users\EWS-01\Downloads\alloma فقط، دون أي تعديل داخل مستودع GitHub.

leading-6" data-expanded="false"><!----> <div class="relative overflow-hidden rounded-xl p-3 bg-[#EAEAEA] dark:bg-[#fff]/10">This is a formal engineering audit of the FireAI Bridge 3: Coverage Proof Certificate module.
As a Senior Fire Protection Auditor, I have performed a high-stress technical validation of the logic, mathematical assumptions, and NFPA 72-2022 code alignment.
EXECUTIVE RISK SUMMARY
The "Mathematical Proof" presented relies on a Pure Geometric Abstraction that fails to account for fluid dynamics and high-ceiling spacing reduction factors required by NFPA 72. While the 
δ
δ
-Conservative Grid logic is mathematically elegant for 2D packing, it creates a False Sense of Safety by claiming "Guaranteed Coverage" without incorporating the Ceiling Height Spacing Reduction Factors (NFPA 72 Table 17.6.3.5.1) or Dead Air Space prohibitions.
TOP 10 AUDIT FINDINGS
1. Underdesign Risk: Failure to Apply Spacing Reduction for High Ceilings
Severity: CRITICAL
Life Safety Impact: Detectors in high-ceiling environments (e.g., warehouses, atria) will be placed too far apart, leading to catastrophic delays in smoke detection and potential loss of life.
Technical Explanation: The code uses a static detector_radius_m (default 6.37m) regardless of height. NFPA 72 §17.6.3.5.1 and Table 17.6.3.5.1 mandate that for ceilings above 10ft (3.0m), the listed spacing (
S
S
) must be reduced (e.g., at 20ft height, spacing is reduced to 64% of listed).
Code Reference: NFPA 72-2022 Table 17.6.3.5.1.
Root Cause: The ProofCertificateGenerator lacks a height-to-spacing lookup table.
Recommended Correction: Implement a mandatory reduction factor method that modifies 
R
R
 based on room_ceiling_height_m before generating the grid.
Confidence Level: High.
2. Failure to Account for "Dead Air" Space (Corner Prohibitions)
Severity: MAJOR
Life Safety Impact: Grid points in corners are "proven" covered by the math, but smoke cannot physically reach these points in a real fire.
Technical Explanation: NFPA 72 §17.6.3.1.3 prohibits smoke detectors from being placed in the 4-inch (100mm) "Dead Air" zone where the wall meets the ceiling. The proof generator treats the room as a perfect polygon up to the edges.
Code Reference: NFPA 72-2022 Figure 17.6.3.1.3.1.
Root Cause: Geometric proof lacks a "physical exclusion zone" for target grid points.
Recommended Correction: Subtract a 100mm buffer from the room perimeter for "Target Points" while maintaining the full perimeter for "Wall Coverage" checks.
Confidence Level: High.
3. Dangerous "Guaranteed" Terminology (Professional Liability)
Severity: MAJOR
Life Safety Impact: AHJs may approve unsafe designs based on the word "Guaranteed," which ignores air flow, stratification, and obstruction.
Technical Explanation: coverage_guaranteed: bool is a binary flag. In fire protection engineering, "coverage" is a probability, never a guarantee. Stratification (NFPA 72 §17.7.3.2.1) can prevent smoke from reaching the ceiling entirely.
Code Reference: NFPA 72-2022 §A.17.7.3.2.1.
Root Cause: Over-reliance on geometric 2D modeling.
Recommended Correction: Rename field to geometric_coverage_proven. Add a mandatory disclaimer to the JSON output regarding stratification and HVAC interference.
Confidence Level: High.
4. Unsafe Area Lower Bound Calculation
Severity: MODERATE
Life Safety Impact: Underestimating the "Max Possible Uncovered Area" leads to a false confidence score in non-perfect layouts.
Technical Explanation: The code calculates uncovered_area_upper_bound_sqm as

Root Cause: Flawed heuristic for area estimation.
Recommended Correction: Use a Voronoi-based area estimation or a simple "Square Grid Cell" area (

5. IEEE 754 Floating Point Hash Instability
Severity: MODERATE
Life Safety Impact: Potential for "Proof Failure" alerts in valid systems due to checksum mismatches across different CPU architectures.
Technical Explanation: compute_hash dumps a dictionary containing floats to JSON. Different Python versions or hardware architectures can serialize 6.37 as 6.3700000000000001 vs 6.37. This breaks the immutable audit trail.
Root Cause: Hashing raw floats instead of quantized strings.
Recommended Correction: Round all floats to a fixed precision (e.g., 4 decimal places) and convert to strings BEFORE hashing.
Confidence Level: Medium.
6. Single Point of Failure: HVAC Coordination
Severity: CRITICAL (Systemic)
Life Safety Impact: A detector placed in the "geometric center" is useless if it is within 3 feet of an HVAC supply air diffuser.
Technical Explanation: The proof claims compliance but does not check for HVAC proximity. NFPA 72 §17.7.4.1 requires detectors to be kept away from supply air.
Code Reference: NFPA 72-2022 §17.7.4.1.
Root Cause: Lack of cross-discipline coordination (Mechanical vs. Fire).
Recommended Correction: The ProofCertificate must accept an obstruction_zones or hvac_zones list to invalidate grid points near air diffusers.
Confidence Level: High.

SYSTEMIC DESIGN WEAKNESSES
Pure 2D Thinking: The system assumes a "flat world." It does not account for beams, joists, or coffered ceilings (NFPA 72 §17.6.3.3), which fundamentally change coverage geometry from circles to "bays."
Stateless Proof: The certificate is only valid at the moment of generation. It lacks a "Model Version" link to the architectural DXF/Revit file, creating a risk where the proof remains "Valid" even if the room dimensions change in the BIM model.
PRIORITY ACTION PLAN

		<script>
			var token = localStorage.getItem('token');
			var base = window.WEBUI_BASE_URL || '';
			var lang = localStorage.locale ?? (navigator.language.includes('zh') ? 'zh-CN' : 'en-US');
			var config = {
				credentials: 'include',
				headers: {
					Accept: 'application/json',
					'Content-Type': 'application/json',
					...(token ? { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } : {}),
					'Accept-Language': lang
				}
			};

			window.GLOBAL_FETCHES = {
				session: fetch(`${base}/api/v1/auths/`, config).then(async (res) => {
					if (res.status === 401) {
						location.replace('/auth');
					}
					if (!res.ok) throw await res.json();
					return res.json();
				}),
				config: fetch(`${base}/api/config`, config).then(async (res) => {
					if (!res.ok) throw await res.json();
					return res.json();
				}),
				models: token && fetch(`${base}/api/models`, config),
				settings:
					token &&
					fetch(`${base}/api/v1/users/user/settings`, config).then(async (res) => {
						if (!res.ok) throw await res.json();
						return res.json();
					})
			};
		</script>
		<script async="" src="//o.alicdn.com/frontend-lib/common-lib/jquery.min.js"></script>
	


<style type="text/css" nonce="">
	html {
		overflow-y: hidden !important;
	}

	#splash-screen {
		background: #f4f6f8;
	}

	html.dark #splash-screen {
		background: #141618;
	}

	#progress-background {
		display: none;
	}

	#progress-bar {
		display: none;
	}

	html.her #logo {
		display: none;
	}

	html.her #progress-background {
		display: block;
	}

	html.her #progress-bar {
		display: block;
	}

	@media (max-width: 24rem) {
		html.her #progress-background {
			display: none;
		}

		html.her #progress-bar {
			display: none;
		}
	}

	@keyframes pulse {
		50% {
			opacity: 0.65;
		}
	}

	.animate-pulse-fast {
		animation: pulse 1.5s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite;
	}
</style>
